title: CPSS — Cyber-Physical Severity Scale: Dataset and Reference Implementation
description:
CPSS (Cyber-Physical Severity Scale) is a logarithmic severity scale for evaluating the physical, environmental, and societal consequences of cyberattacks against Industrial Control Systems (ICS).

CPSS bridges the gap between existing metrics: CVSS covers software vulnerability exploitability, HAZOP/SIL covers industrial process safety, and INES covers nuclear accidents only. CPSS integrates the full chain from cyber intrusion → physical accident → societal impact into a single deterministic score.

Formula

CPSS = max(H, E, I, C)

  • H (Human): log10(deaths × 100 + hospitalized × 10 + 1)
  • E (Environmental): log10(area_m² × P / 10)
  • I (Infrastructure): log10(population / 1000 + 1)
  • C (Economic): log10(loss_usd / 100000)

Repository Contents

  • dataset/cpss_incidents.csv — 100 documented ICS cyber incidents (2000–2024), covering power, water, oil & gas, manufacturing, and transportation sectors. Each entry includes primary source reference, source type (academic / government / vendor report / news), and confidence level.
  • src/cpss_calculator.py — Reference implementation of the CPSS formula with CLI interface.
  • docs/cpss_spec.md — Full specification including scoring rubrics, calibration examples, and dataset inclusion criteria.
  • paper/cpss_arxiv.tex — LaTeX source of the accompanying arXiv preprint.
